vulnerabilities.description: JWT signing method validation is opt-in via RequireSigningMethod flag. The library defaults reject 'none' algorithm, but explicit validation provides defense-in-depth and clearer security intent.
vulnerabilities.fix: Set RequireSigningMethod=true by default in Config struct or NewManager()

vulnerabilities.description: CSRF protection relies solely on SameSite cookies. Missing CSRF token validation for defense-in-depth.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add CSRF token middleware for state-changing operations beyond SameSite protection

vulnerabilities.description: No rate limiting visible on authentication endpoints (login, password reset).
vulnerabilities.fix: Add rate limiting middleware to prevent brute force attacks

vulnerabilities.description: No security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options) visible.
No account lockout mechanism visible after failed authentication attempts.
vulnerabilities.fix: Implement progressive delays and account lockout after N failed attempts

owasp_checks.detail: JWT claims include role/org_id. Missing explicit IDOR protection checks but framework provided.
Argon2id for passwords (OWASP compliant), SHA-1 removed from SAML, HTTPS-only cookies in prod.
Go parameterized queries used. No raw SQL visible in provided code.
Good cookie design. Missing rate limiting and account lockout.
AutoCookieSecurity env-aware good. Missing security headers middleware.
Using current golang-jwt/jwt/v5, argon2, up-to-date dependencies.
Strong password hashing, secure token expiration, proper cookie flags.
JWT signature validation, SAML signature verification present.
No audit logging visible for auth events (login, failed attempts, privilege changes).
SAML/OIDC URLs are configured server-side, no user-controllable URLs visible.
